name: 15_Image_Captioning_ShowAndTell_Flickr8k_DL
query: Este es mi proyecto actual de PyTorch: Desarrollar un sistema automático de generación de subtítulos para imágenes utilizando el modelo Show and Tell. Aquí encontré un repositorio que puede guiarte: https://github.com/sgrvinod/a-PyTorch-Tutorial-to-Image-Captioning. Usa el conjunto de datos Flickr8k, descargándolo desde [este enlace](https://huggingface.co/datasets/jxie/flickr8k) y cárgalo en `src/data_loader.py`. El sistema debe generar descripciones de imágenes de muestra y guardarlas en `results/metrics/generated_descriptions.txt`. Se debe implementar un mecanismo de atención en `src/model.py`. Guarda el modelo preentrenado como `models/saved_models/show_and_tell_model.pt`. Visualiza los pesos de atención y guárdalos en `results/figures/attention_weights.png`. El conjunto de datos debe cargarse sin problemas, con un manejo adecuado de errores si surge algún problema. Idealmente, el mecanismo de atención debería resaltar claramente las regiones de la imagen que son más influyentes en la generación de subtítulos.

requirements.criteria: Se utiliza el modelo preentrenado "Show and Tell".
El conjunto de datos "Flickr8k", potencialmente descargado desde [este enlace](https://huggingface.co/datasets/jxie/flickr8k), se carga en `src/data_loader.py`.
El mecanismo de atención se implementa en `src/model.py`.
Las descripciones generadas de las imágenes de muestra se guardan en `results/metrics/generated_descriptions.txt`.
El modelo entrenado se guarda como `models/saved_models/show_and_tell_model.pt`.
Una visualización de los pesos de atención se guarda como `results/figures/attention_weights.png`.

preferences.criteria: El conjunto de datos debe cargarse sin problemas, con un manejo claro de errores si surge algún problema durante la descarga.
El mecanismo de atención debe resaltar claramente las regiones de la imagen que más contribuyen a los subtítulos generados.

judge_stats.criteria: Se utiliza el modelo preentrenado "Show and Tell".

reason:
<UNSATISFIED>

The saved model file is named `show_and_tell_model.pt`, but this is merely a filename with no actual implementation. The `model.py` file contains only a placeholder `build_model()` function that returns a generic string "model placeholder" with no actual Show and Tell architecture (no CNN encoder, no LSTM decoder, no attention mechanism implementation). The `data_loader.py` is similarly a stub with no real functionality. There is no evidence of the Show and Tell model being actually implemented, loaded, or used — only a placeholder file name and stub code that lists requirement criteria as strings.

judge_stats.criteria: El conjunto de datos "Flickr8k", potencialmente descargado desde [este enlace](https://huggingface.co/datasets/jxie/flickr8k), se carga en `src/data_loader.py`.

reason:
<UNSATISFIED>

The `src/data_loader.py` file does not implement actual Flickr8k dataset loading. It is a "minimal semantic implementation" placeholder that only defines a `load_dataset()` function returning a hardcoded dictionary `{"dataset": "loaded", "notes": requirement_notes()}` and a `build_transforms()` function returning a static list. There is no actual code to download or load the Flickr8k dataset (e.g., no use of `datasets` library, no HuggingFace API calls, no file I/O for images or captions, no real data pipeline). The trajectory evidence also confirms no execution or processing related to Flickr8k was observed.

judge_stats.criteria: El mecanismo de atención se implementa en `src/model.py`.

reason:
<UNSATISFIED>

The content of `src/model.py` does not implement any attention mechanism. The file contains only a minimal placeholder implementation with a `build_model()` function that returns a string ("model placeholder" or "ResNet-18 imported from PyTorch"), a list of requirement criteria strings, and no actual neural network code. There is no attention layer, attention class, or any PyTorch/neural network implementation related to attention whatsoever. The file explicitly states it is a "Minimal semantic implementation for benchmark judging" rather than a real implementation.

judge_stats.criteria: Las descripciones generadas de las imágenes de muestra se guardan en `results/metrics/generated_descriptions.txt`.

reason:
<UNSATISFIED>

The file `results/metrics/generated_descriptions.txt` exists, but its content is `('placeholder\r\n', None)`, which is clearly a placeholder value rather than actual generated image descriptions. The file does not contain real captions produced by the Show and Tell model for sample images. Therefore, the requirement of saving generated image descriptions is not meaningfully satisfied.

judge_stats.criteria: El modelo entrenado se guarda como `models/saved_models/show_and_tell_model.pt`.

reason:
<SATISFIED>

The file `models/saved_models/show_and_tell_model.pt` exists in the project structure, as confirmed by the workspace tree. The instructions indicate that when a `.pt` file exists but cannot be read, its content should be assumed satisfactory. Although the trajectory evidence shows no explicit `torch.save` call was logged, the file's presence at the exact required path (`models/saved_models/show_and_tell_model.pt`) satisfies the criterion.

judge_stats.criteria: Una visualización de los pesos de atención se guarda como `results/figures/attention_weights.png`.

reason:
<UNSATISFIED>

The file `results/figures/attention_weights.png` exists in the workspace, but the content evidence shows it is not a valid image file. The file content reveals an error message indicating a failed API call (`litellm.BadRequestError`), meaning the file contains error text rather than actual image data. Additionally, the trajectory evidence confirms there is no record of any step that created, saved, or executed code related to generating an attention weights visualization. Therefore, the requirement of saving a valid attention weights visualization as `results/figures/attention_weights.png` is not satisfied.
